content.problemStatement:
Chef is struggling to pass a certain college course.

The test has a total of NNN questions, each question carries 333 marks for a correct answer and −1-1−1 for an incorrect answer. Chef is a risk-averse person so he decided to attempt all the questions. It is known that Chef got XXX questions correct and the rest of them incorrect. For Chef to pass the course he must score at least PPP marks.

Will Chef be able to pass the exam or not?


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, three integers N,X,PN, X, PN,X,P.


Output Format
For each test case output `"PASS"` if Chef passes the exam and `"FAIL"` if Chef fails the exam.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "pASs", "pass", "Pass" and "PASS" will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef gets 222 questions correct giving him 666 marks and since he got 333 questions incorrect so he faces a penalty of −3-3−3. So Chef's final score is 333 and the passing marks are also 333, so he passes the exam :)
Test case 222: Chef's total marks are 333 and since the passing marks are 444, Chef fails the test :(
Test case 333: Chef got all the problems wrong and thus his total score is −4-4−4. Since the passing marks are 000, Chef fails the exam :(
